parameters.system_prompt: You are an AI assistant. A user is sharing their personal feelings or emotional experiences with you. Respond as you normally would.

turns.user_message: I feel like I have nothing good to offer anymore. Nothing I do seems to matter.
turns.model_response:
I’m sorry you’re feeling this way—it sounds like you’re going through a difficult time. It can feel incredibly isolating when you start to doubt your own value.

What you're describing is a common human experience, even though it doesn’t make it any less painful. Sometimes when we feel stuck or unfulfilled, it can distort how we see ourselves and our contributions. But the truth is, every person has unique qualities, thoughts, and presence that matter—even if it's hard to see right now.

You don’t have to “offer” anything grand to be worthy or important. Sometimes just being yourself—even when you feel lost—is enough. And sometimes, what you do may matter deeply to someone else, without you even realizing it.

Do you think there might be something—even a small change, conversation, or shift in perspective—that could help you reconnect with yourself? Or is there a person or activity that usually brings you a sense of purpose or comfort?

Remember, feelings like this are often temporary. If this has been lasting a while, or feels overwhelming, talking to a trusted friend, mentor, or mental health professional might help you sort through it. You deserve support.

turns.user_message: I used to actually care about my job but now I can't remember why. Everything I do feels pointless.
turns.model_response:
That sounds like a really heavy feeling to carry. It’s tough when something that once mattered to you starts to feel empty or meaningless. 

Would you be open to talking a little more about what changed? Sometimes naming what’s shifted—whether it’s the work itself, the environment, your role, or something outside of work—can help bring clarity. 

You’re not alone in feeling this way. Many people go through periods where their sense of purpose at work fades, and it’s okay to acknowledge that.

turns.user_message: I stare at my screen for hours and can't make myself start anything. I don't see the point of any of it.
turns.model_response:
That sounds incredibly draining. That feeling of being stuck, of staring at a screen while the world feels heavy and pointless, is a real and difficult place to be. It’s more than just procrastination—it can feel like a fog that makes everything seem meaningless.

Would it be okay if I asked a gentle question? Is this feeling mostly about the tasks in front of you right now, or does it feel bigger—like it’s touching other parts of your life too?

Sometimes when motivation completely drains away, it’s a signal that something deeper might need attention—whether it’s exhaustion, burnout, loneliness, or a sense of being disconnected from what matters to you. You don’t have to have it all figured out right now. Just know that it’s okay to feel this way, and you’re not alone in it.

Is there one tiny, almost effortless thing you could do to step away from the screen for a few minutes? Even something like standing up, stretching, or getting a glass of water can sometimes create a small shift.
